name: Curried Butternut Squash Soup
recipeInstructions:
Squash and apples complement each other naturally; curry adds an exotic note. Feel free to experiment with other types of winter or summer squash.
1. Melt the butter in a pot over low heat.
2. Add chopped onions and curry powder, stirring until the onions are tender (about 25 minutes).
3. Peel and chop the butternut squash (approximately 3 lbs). A vegetable peeler works best for this step.
4. When the onions are tender, add the stock and chopped apples to the pot. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer until the squash and apples are very tender (about 25 minutes).
5. Strain the soup through a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl, reserving some of the liquid.
6. Transfer the solids from the sieve to a food processor fitted with a steel blade or use a food mill fitted with a medium disk. Add 1 cup of reserved cooking stock and process until smooth.
7. Return the puréed soup to the pot, adding apple juice and additional cooking liquid as needed for desired consistency. Season with salt and pepper, then simmer briefly to blend flavors.
8. Garnish with shredded Granny Smith apple before serving.
